changelog: Replaced AirLLM path with ada-llama-cpp-local (real llama.cpp + GGUF). Self-Hosted AI Pack now documents verified benchmarks: 108 t/s on GPU, 17 t/s on CPU, $0/month. Catalog: 11 agents, 8 packs, 49 skills.

skill_packs.name: Operator Starter Pack
skill_packs.tagline: Fresh box → running agent in 10 minutes.
features: Provision a new box in 10 minutes (script + post-install verification)
Replicate a fully-loaded agent to a remote machine
Sync skills and memory across multiple boxes
Encrypted, automated off-site backups to Google Drive
Local encrypted vault for API keys and notes
Generate branded PDFs from raw data
LAN-only web UI access for the agent's dashboard
Human-speed browser for sites without an API
use_cases: You're setting up your first agent on a new machine and want it identical to a friend's
You run 2+ boxes and want them to stay in sync overnight
You don't want a disk crash to lose months of agent memory

skill_packs.name: Self-Hosted AI Pack
skill_packs.tagline: Real OpenAI-compatible AI on YOUR hardware. 108 t/s on GPU, 17 t/s on CPU. $0/month, forever.
features: Installs llama.cpp (90k+★, MIT) and downloads a Qwen 2.5 GGUF from HuggingFace
OpenAI-compatible API on http://localhost:11434/v1 — drop-in replacement, no code changes
VERIFIED BENCHMARKS: 108.7 tok/s on GPU (qwen2.5-3b-instruct-q4_k_m.gguf, 0.24s wall time)
VERIFIED BENCHMARKS: 16.7 tok/s on CPU (qwen2.5-1.5b-instruct-q8_0.gguf, 1.1–1.5s wall time)
One-command start: llama-server -m model.gguf -c 2048 --host 0.0.0.0 --port 11434
Works on any 4 GB+ GPU, any modern CPU, any Apple Silicon Mac
Verified install report with hardware tier, model, tok/s, and a real sample response
use_cases: Privacy-first AI for sensitive business data — nothing ever leaves your box
Run AI offline during travel, remote work, or in the field
Slash AI costs from $100/month to $0 after the one-time install
Use the MacBook M2 you already own — llama.cpp has first-class Metal support
Chat with confidential docs without uploading them anywhere
skill_packs.deliverable: 4 skills · llama-cpp-install.sh · download-model.sh · serve-llama-cpp.sh · verify-install.sh · install report
skill_packs.who_its_for: Developers, privacy-conscious companies, hobbyists with a GPU, VPS owners, and anyone who refuses to send data to OpenAI

agents.name: Crypto Trading Agent
agents.description: Tracks exchange accounts (Kraken, Coinbase, Binance) via read-only API keys, applies configurable rules, and posts signals to Telegram or Discord. Includes backtesting, position-sizing, and drawdown guards. Skills-only — bring your own strategy and persona.
features: Read-only exchange API (Kraken, Coinbase, Binance)
Custom rule engine — entry/exit/stop
Telegram + Discord signal dispatch
Drawdown guard + position sizing
Daily P/L digest to email

agents.name: Customer Support Agent
agents.tagline: Tier-1 support that knows your docs.
agents.description: Embeddable chat widget + email responder trained on a configured knowledge base. Auto-routes to humans on edge cases. Multi-language out of the box. Skills-only — fully neutral, no pre-set tone or persona.
features: Knowledge-base grounded answers
Multi-language (EN, ES, FR, DE, JP)
Edge-case routing to humans
Email + chat + Slack support
Sentiment + escalation tracking

agents.name: Social Media Manager Agent
agents.tagline: Schedule, post, reply, and report — across 8 networks.

features: 8 networks (X, LinkedIn, Mastodon, Bluesky, Threads, FB, IG, YT)
Content calendar with approval flow
Auto-reply to mentions / DMs
Weekly analytics report (PDF + email)
Hashtag + trend suggestion

agents.name: Code Review Agent
agents.tagline: PRs reviewed in under 90 seconds. Catches what humans miss.
agents.description: Watches GitHub/GitLab repos. On every PR: runs static analysis, checks for security smells, posts diff-aware review comments. Learns the configured team style. Skills-only — no embedded reviewer persona, fully configurable.
features: GitHub + GitLab + Bitbucket PR review
Security smell detection (SQLi, XSS, secrets)
Style-aware inline comments
Auto-approve green PRs, alert on red
Slack/Teams notification digest

agents.name: Email Triage Agent
agents.tagline: Inbox zero, automatically. Keeps what matters, drafts the rest.
agents.description: Connects to Gmail/Outlook via OAuth. Classifies incoming email, drafts replies in the configured voice, surfaces urgent threads, and archives noise. Human stays in control — agent suggests, user approves. Skills-only — no embedded persona.
features: Gmail + Outlook + Fastmail OAuth
Priority inbox with reasoning shown
Reply drafts in the configured voice
Unsubscribe automation (CAN-SPAM safe)
Daily digest: 1 line, the only line that matters

agents.name: Research Agent
agents.tagline: Web search → cited briefing in 5 minutes.
agents.description: Plans a research question, runs multi-source searches, deduplicates findings, and writes a cited brief in the configured format. Skills-only — no embedded editorial voice, user controls style and length.
features: Multi-source web search with deduplication
Inline citations pulled from a reference manager
Briefings in user-configured format (1-pager, memo, paper)
Export to Markdown, PDF, or BibTeX
Topic digest on a cron schedule

agents.name: Content Creator Agent
agents.tagline: Image, video, audio, and design — from a single brief.
agents.description: Reads a launch brief, then produces a hero image, a diagram, a short video, and a reference audio in a chosen style. Skills-only — no embedded voice, user defines brand and tone.
features: Hero image in on-brand style (ComfyUI pipeline)
Hand-drawn diagram from outline (Excalidraw)
Short explainer video from script (Manim)
Original song / jingle with reference audio (songwriting)
Brand-aligned UI/UX mocks (Claude Design)

agents.name: Private AI Assistant
agents.tagline: Your own ChatGPT, on your hardware, private.
agents.description: A self-hosted AI assistant that runs entirely on the user's box — no data leaves the machine, no monthly API bill, and real measured throughput: 108.7 tok/s on GPU, 16.7 tok/s on CPU. Backed by llama.cpp + Qwen 2.5 GGUF (1.5B Q8 for CPU, 3B Q4_K_M for GPU), with first-class Apple Silicon / Metal support. Exposes an OpenAI-compatible endpoint so any existing tool (desktop app, web UI, curl, custom script) drops in unchanged. Skills-only — fully blank, user defines persona and prompts.
features: Runs entirely on your hardware (4GB+ GPU, any modern CPU, Apple Silicon)
No data ever leaves your machine — zero telemetry
Verified 108.7 tok/s on GPU, 16.7 tok/s on CPU — $0/month after install
OpenAI-compatible API on http://localhost:11434/v1
Encrypted backups of conversations and settings
Drop-in replacement for OpenAI in any existing tool
use_cases: Confidential docs — chat with proprietary material without uploading
Offline AI — works on a plane, in the field, or behind a strict firewall
Zero monthly cost — replace your $100/month OpenAI bill after one install
